```json
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "Textbook SQL injection via unsanitized user input directly interpolated into query string"
  },

  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "SQL Injection via f-string query interpolation",
      "risk": "Full database compromise — attacker can read, modify, or delete any data, and potentially execute OS commands depending on DB engine",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "search_deliveries(), lines 2-6 (query construction)",
      "reproduction": "Call endpoint with customer_id='; DROP TABLE deliveries;-- or status_filter=' OR '1'='1 to dump all rows. A crafted UNION SELECT can exfiltrate other tables (users, credentials, payments).",
      "fix": "Use parameterized queries: db_conn.execute(\"SELECT * FROM deliveries WHERE customer_id = ? AND status = ?\", (customer_id, status_filter)). Adjust placeholder syntax (? vs %s vs :name) per DB driver.",
      "time_to_fix": "5 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Security: no user-supplied input may reach SQL without parameterization"
    },
    {
      "title": "No input validation on customer_id or status_filter",
      "risk": "Even with parameterized queries, unbounded or malformed input can cause unexpected behavior — empty strings return wrong results, excessively long strings cause performance issues, and missing authorization allows horizontal privilege escalation (any user can query any customer_id)",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "search_deliveries() function signature / call site",
      "reproduction": "Call with customer_id of a different user — no authorization check exists. Call with status_filter='' or a 10MB string.",
      "fix": "Validate customer_id matches the authenticated user's ID (authorization check). Validate status_filter against an allowlist of known statuses (e.g., 'pending', 'shipped', 'delivered'). Reject or truncate oversized input.",
      "time_to_fix": "15 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Authorization: users must only access their own deliveries"
    },
    {
      "title": "SELECT * returns unbounded result set with no pagination",
      "risk": "A customer with thousands of deliveries (or an attacker who bypasses filters) triggers a full table scan returned into memory, causing OOM or denial of service",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "search_deliveries(), query and fetchall()",
      "reproduction": "Query a customer_id with 100k+ deliveries, or exploit the SQL injection to remove the WHERE clause entirely. fetchall() loads every row into memory at once.",
      "fix": "Add LIMIT/OFFSET pagination to the query. Replace fetchall() with batched iteration or fetchmany(). Avoid SELECT * — enumerate only needed columns.",
      "time_to_fix": "15 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Reliability: endpoint must remain responsive under adversarial or high-volume conditions"
    }
  ],

  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "SELECT * is fragile and leaks internal columns",
      "description": "SELECT * returns every column including potential internal fields (internal_notes, cost_price, driver_ssn). Schema changes silently alter the API response shape, breaking clients.",
      "location": "search_deliveries(), query"
    },
    {
      "title": "No error handling around db_conn.execute",
      "description": "Database timeouts, connection drops, or malformed queries will propagate raw exceptions to the caller — potentially leaking schema details in a 500 response to the public API.",
      "location": "search_deliveries(), execute/fetchall calls"
    }
  ],

  "suggestions": [
    "Wrap DB calls in try/except and return a sanitized error to the API layer",
    "Add logging for query execution time and result count for observability",
    "Consider an ORM or query builder to make parameterization the default path"
  ],

  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "This code is actively exploitable the moment it receives traffic. Any deployment exposes the entire database.",
    "rollback_capability": "Rollback is straightforward — revert the endpoint. However, if exploited before rollback, data may already be compromised.",
    "backward_compatibility": "Fixing the SQL injection (parameterized queries) is backward-compatible for legitimate callers. Adding authorization and validation may reject previously-accepted malformed requests.",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No logging or metrics — exploitation would be invisible without external WAF or DB audit logs.",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": false,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": true,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": ["auth_security: endpoint has zero authorization — any caller can query any customer's deliveries"]
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Revert the endpoint deployment. Audit DB access logs for injection patterns. If evidence of exploitation, initiate incident response."
  },

  "escalations": [],

  "confidence": {
    "level": 99,
    "high_confidence_areas": ["SQL injection is textbook and unambiguous", "Missing authorization is evident from the code", "Unbounded fetchall is a clear DoS vector"],
    "low_confidence_areas": ["Exact DB driver placeholder syntax depends on runtime environment"],
    "assumptions": ["db_conn follows Python DB-API 2.0 (PEP 249)", "customer_id and status_filter arrive unsanitized from HTTP params as stated"]
  }
}
```
